message:
test(symbol-drift): treat reverse drift (extern "C" fn missing matrix entry) as informational

The abi_source_fns_have_matrix_entries test in symbol_drift_test.rs
previously hard-asserted that every `extern "C" fn` in the ABI source
had a matching support_matrix.json entry, which made adding a new
ABI symbol always fail this test until the matrix was regenerated in
the same commit. That created an artificial ordering constraint —
new ports could not land independently from the matrix refresh, even
though scripts/check_symbol_drift.sh in the CI gate already classifies
reverse drift as informational (forward drift, where the matrix
references a symbol the ABI does not export, remains a hard error).

This commit aligns the unit test with the CI gate: orphan symbols are
printed via eprintln! with a count and the explanatory note that the
shell gate treats reverse drift as informational, but the test no
longer fails on them. Forward drift (matrix entry without an
extern "C" fn behind it) is still asserted hard elsewhere in the
file.

Module doc comment updated to document the new contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
